title: Role
description: A role contract: a controlled job function with declared inputs, outputs, allowed tools, forbidden actions, required gates, escalation paths, and permission flags. Roles are the unit of accountability; a workflow step always pins to exactly one role.

id.description: Namespaced role identifier in kebab/snake case. Format: <guild>.<role-slug>. Example: engineering.implementation.

guild.description: Owning guild. One of the twelve guilds named in ops/control-plane.md. The commercial guild is reserved for revenue-adjacent roles (pricing, packaging, subscriber experience) that don't fit product or design cleanly.

mission.description: Single-sentence statement of what this role exists to do.

inputs.description: Input contracts the role consumes. Order is informational only.

required.description: Whether the role refuses to start without this input.

outputs.description: Output contracts the role produces. Each output names an artifact type from artifact.schema.json.

artifact_type.description: Artifact type. Must match the enum in artifact.schema.json.

required.description: Whether the role's completion check rejects the run if this output is missing.

allowed_tools.description: Tool identifiers (matching the id pattern in tool.schema.json) the role is permitted to invoke. Empty array means a read-only role.

forbidden_actions.description: Free-form list of actions the role must not perform, used by the policy engine as a defense-in-depth deny list. Example values: deploy_to_production, modify_secrets, approve_own_work.

required_gates.description: Names of proof gates that must pass before the role's run is marked done. Workflows reference these names from steps.gate.

escalation.description: Escalation paths. When a condition fires, the run hands off to escalate_to (another role id).

condition.description: Free-form condition that triggers the handoff. Example: gate_failed_twice or budget_exceeded.

permissions.description: Boolean permission flags. The policy engine evaluates these as the first cut before consulting policy rules.

write_code.description: May write or modify code files.

approve.description: May grant approvals (PR approval, gate override, release sign-off).

promote_memory.description: May promote dream candidates into long-term memory.

runtime_hint.description: Preferred runtime for executing this role. Informational; the harness may override.

memory_policy_file.description: Path to the role's memory-policy.yaml, relative to the owning repo root.

examples_dir.description: Path to a directory of example runs or prompts for the role, relative to the owning repo root.
